codeReview.summary: Pass: the commit is limited to formatting changes in `update.rs`, `local_models.rs`, and `search_service.rs`. Manual diff review shows only line wrapping/whitespace adjustments, and a token-normalized comparison of each file against the parent commit found no non-whitespace changes, so the prior local-inference scrutiny fixes are not regressed.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `implementation-worker` skill is written for substantive Rust feature work and still requires red-green tests, `cargo test`, `cargo clippy`, and manual CLI verification even for a formatting-only follow-up. This session appropriately limited itself to `cargo fmt`/`cargo fmt --check`, but the handoff still marked `skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true`, suggesting the shared skill guidance is broader than the task type it was used for.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/skills/implementation-worker/SKILL.md:14,47,80,86,88,94,116` requires failing tests first plus `cargo test`, `cargo clippy`, `cargo fmt --check`, and manual verification, while the `local-fmt-fix` transcript/handoff records only `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo fmt --all --check`, and the commit command.

summary: Reviewed `local-fmt-fix` commit `435e5a4d529e7530047357fbc44239b6d9303298`: it is formatting-only, keeps the prior local-inference scrutiny fixes intact, and passes review. The only follow-up note is a shared-state/skill mismatch where the general implementation-worker procedure is stricter than this formatting-only task required.
